refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms in communication protocol.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to reduce effective packet loss.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms in communication protocol.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality control within the communication protocol, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Risk of incomplete error correction under extreme network degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or redundant channels if adaptive bitrate alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time interaction with the vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Incoming telemetry data from the vehicle used to monitor communication quality.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands sent to the vehicle based on teleoperation control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily executes commands; limited role in communication quality control.
Highest containment score; centralizes communication quality management and teleoperation control.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not manage communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing packet loss management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module responsibilities.
Risk of tight coupling with network link interface affecting modularity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module complexity and consider splitting communication quality functions if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive bitrate and error correction) directly addresses the refined FR with clear verification methods and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity concentration in Teleoperation module.
Dependency on network link interface may require robust interface management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via telemetry and network link interfaces within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for packet loss management to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider fallback or redundancy strategies in DP to mitigate risks.
Review Teleoperation module complexity in future design iterations.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirement with acceptable risk and clear verification paths. No further refinement needed at this stage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

revise.why: Current artifacts meet criteria; no revision required.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms are effective under expected conditions.
open_risks: Latency introduced by adaptive algorithms may affect teleoperation responsiveness.
Extreme network degradation scenarios not fully mitigated.
evidence_needed: Performance test results showing packet loss under various network conditions.
Latency measurements during teleoperation with adaptive bitrate enabled.
